task: Use the Scanorama toolkit to perform batch correction on multiple datasets and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected datasets.
Use the Scanorama toolkit to perform batch correction on multiple datasets and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected datasets.
Use the Scanorama toolkit to perform batch correction on multiple datasets and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected datasets.
Use the Scanorama toolkit to perform batch correction on multiple datasets and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected datasets.
Use the Scanorama toolkit to perform batch correction on multiple datasets and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected datasets.
Use the Scanorama toolkit to perform batch correction on multiple datasets and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected datasets.
Use the Harmony toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected dataset.
Use the Harmony toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset, set the max_iter_harmony parameter to 15, and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected dataset.

Use the Harmony toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset, and output how much the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected dataset has increased compared to the average iLiSi of the original uncorrected dataset (calculate their difference).
Use the Harmony toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset and output the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected dataset.

Use the Harmony toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset, modify the max_iter_harmony parameter, and run results with values of 1 and 2 respectively. Output how much the average iLiSi of the dataset after iter2 batch correction has increased compared to the average iLiSi of the dataset after iter1 batch correction (calculate their difference).

Use the Harmony toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset, and output how much the average iLiSi of the batch-corrected dataset has increased compared to the average iLiSi of the original uncorrected dataset (calculate their difference).

Use the BBKNN toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset with default parameters (neighbors_within_batch=3), and output the iLiSi of the UMAP embedding generated from the corrected graph.
Use the BBKNN toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset, set neighbors_within_batch to 5, and output the iLiSi of the UMAP embedding.
Use the BBKNN toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset, set neighbors_within_batch to 10, and output the iLiSi of the UMAP embedding.
Use the BBKNN toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset, set trim to 20, and output the iLiSi of the UMAP embedding.
Use the BBKNN toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset with approx=False (using exact neighbors), and output the iLiSi of the UMAP embedding.

Use the BBKNN toolkit to perform batch correction on the PCA-reduced dataset twice: once with neighbors_within_batch=10 and once with neighbors_within_batch=3. Calculate the absolute difference in iLiSi of the UMAP embeddings between these two settings.
Use the BBKNN toolkit to perform batch correction with default parameters, and output the mean connectivity of the resulting neighbor graph (mean of the 'connectivities' matrix).
Use the BBKNN toolkit with metric='euclidean', and output the iLiSi of the UMAP embedding.
Use the scVI toolkit to train a model on the raw count dataset with default parameters (max_epochs=20), and output the final training ELBO.
Use the scVI toolkit to train a model on the raw count dataset (max_epochs=20), extract the latent representation (get_latent_representation), and output the iLiSi of this latent space.
Use the scVI toolkit to train a model with n_latent=5 (max_epochs=20), and output the final training ELBO.
Use the scVI toolkit to train a model with n_latent=30 (max_epochs=20), and output the final training ELBO.
Use the scVI toolkit to train a model with n_layers=2 (max_epochs=20), and output the final training ELBO.
Use the scVI toolkit to train a model with gene_likelihood='nb' (Negative Binomial) and max_epochs=20, and output the final training ELBO.

Use the scVI toolkit to train a model (max_epochs=20), and calculate the Reconstruction Loss (MSE) for the gene 'ENSG00000166848' by comparing the model's normalized expression to the raw data's library-size normalized expression.
Use the scVI toolkit to train a model with n_hidden=256 (max_epochs=20), and output the final training ELBO.
Use the scVI toolkit to train a model (max_epochs=20), get the normalized expression, and output the mean expression value of the gene 'ENSG00000166848' across all cells.

Using the pretrained "Immune_All_Low.pkl" model from the Celltypist toolkit, perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset" with all parameters set to default, and finally output the predicted type of the 1726th (row) cell in the annotated data.

Using the pretrained "Immune_All_Low.pkl" model from the Celltypist toolkit, perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset" with all parameters kept default, and finally output whether the predicted type of the 1055th (row) cell in the annotated data matches the true type (True or False).

Using the pretrained "Immune_All_Low.pkl" model from the Celltypist toolkit, perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset" with all parameters kept default, and finally output whether the predicted type of the 378th (row) cell in the annotated data matches the true type (True or False).

Using the pretrained "Immune_All_Low.pkl" model from the Celltypist toolkit with default parameters, perform single-cell type annotation on the "test dataset". After annotation, count how many cells are predicted as "gamma-delta T cells". Output this integer count.

Using the pretrained "Immune_All_Low.pkl" model (default params) on the "test dataset", identify specific misclassifications. Count how many cells have a ground truth of "Macrophages" but were incorrectly predicted as "Alveolar macrophages". Output the integer count.

Use the Celltypist toolkit to train on the "train dataset", then perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset" with all parameters kept default, and finally output the predicted type of the 378th (row) cell in the annotation data.

Use the Celltypist toolkit to train on the "train dataset", then perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset" with all parameters kept default, and finally output whether the predicted type of the 51th (row) cell in the annotated data matches the true type (True or False).

Use the Celltypist toolkit to train on the "train dataset", then perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset" with all parameters kept default, and finally output whether the predicted type of the 38th (row) cell in the annotated data matches the true type (True or False).

Using the pretrained "Immune_All_Low.pkl" model from the Celltypist toolkit with default parameters, perform single-cell type annotation on the "test dataset". After annotation, count how many cells are predicted as "Mast cells". Output this integer count.

Using the pretrained "Immune_All_Low.pkl" model (default params) on the "test dataset", identify specific misclassifications. Count how many cells have a ground truth of "Plasma cells" but were incorrectly predicted as "gamma-delta T cells". Output the integer count.

Use the SVM to train on the "train dataset", then perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset". Set the random seed to 42, and output the final prediction accuracy. Output a value between 0 and 1.

Use the SVM to train on the "train dataset", then perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset". Set the random seed to 42, and output the final prediction F1-Score. Output a value between 0 and 1.

Use the SVM to train on the "train dataset", then perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset". Set the random seed to 42, and output whether cell92 is predicted correctly (True or False).
Use the SVM to train on the "train dataset", then perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset". Set the random seed to 42, and output the predicted type of cell18.
Use the SVM to train on the "train dataset", then perform single-cell type annotation for the "test dataset". Set the random seed to 42, and output the predicted type of cell77.

Preprocess the datasets by selecting the top 2000 highly variable genes (Seurat v3 flavor). Using scANVI from scvi-tools, train on the concatenated dataset (train labeled, test unlabeled), set random seed to 0, use default hyperparameters (10 epochs for scVI, 10 epochs for scANVI for speed in demo). Output the prediction accuracy on the "test dataset". Output a float between 0 and 1.

Preprocess the datasets by selecting the top 2000 highly variable genes (Seurat v3 flavor). Using scANVI (seed=0, defaults), predict cell types for the "test dataset". Output the predicted cell type of the 10th cell (index 9) in the test set.

Preprocess the datasets by selecting the top 2000 highly variable genes (Seurat v3 flavor). Using scANVI (seed=0, defaults), predict cell types for the "test dataset". Count how many cells are predicted as "B cells". Output the integer count.

Preprocess the datasets by selecting the top 2000 highly variable genes (Seurat v3 flavor). Using scANVI (seed=0, defaults), check if the predicted label for the 50th cell (index 49) in the "test dataset" matches its ground truth label. Output True or False.

Preprocess the datasets by selecting the top 2000 highly variable genes (Seurat v3 flavor). Using scANVI (seed=0, defaults), identify misclassifications. Count how many cells have a ground truth of "NK cells" but were incorrectly predicted as "T cells". Output the integer count.
Use Random Forest (sklearn, n_estimators=100, random_state=42) trained on the raw expression matrix of "train dataset" to predict "test dataset". Output the accuracy. Output a float between 0 and 1.
Use Random Forest (sklearn, n_estimators=100, random_state=42) trained on "train dataset" to predict "test dataset". Output the predicted label of the 200th cell (index 199).
Use Random Forest (sklearn, n_estimators=100, random_state=42). Output whether the prediction for the 300th cell (index 299) matches the ground truth (True or False).
Use Random Forest (sklearn, n_estimators=100, random_state=42). Count how many cells are predicted as 'Endothelial cells'. Output the integer count.

Use Random Forest (sklearn, n_estimators=100, random_state=42). Identify the number of cells that are truly 'Monocytes' but were predicted as any other type (false negatives for Monocytes). Output the integer count.

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Retrieve the 20-dimensional latent representation. Calculate the mean value of the first dimension (index 0) across all 400 cells.

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Predict the denoised protein expression (n_samples=10). Calculate the average expression value of protein 'CD4' across all cells.

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Calculate the 'foreground probability' (n_samples=10) for protein 'CD8a'. Return the median probability across all cells.

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Calculate the Pearson correlation between the denoised protein expression of 'CD3_TotalSeqB' (n_samples=10) and the log-normalized RNA expression of gene 'CD3E'.

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Perform Leiden clustering (resolution=0.5) on the TotalVI latent space. How many distinct cell clusters are identified?

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Compute the pairwise Euclidean distance in the TotalVI latent space between the first cell (index 0) and the second cell (index 1).

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Calculate the log-fold change (LFC) of denoised 'CD19' protein expression (n_samples=10) between the first 50 cells and the last 50 cells.

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Retrieve denoised protein expression for 'CD56' (n_samples=10). Calculate the variance of this expression across the 400 cells.

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Denoise the RNA expression (n_samples=10) and calculate the maximum denoised expression value found for gene 'MS4A1' across all cells.

Load the PBMC CITE-seq dataset, subsample it to 400 cells (random_state=42), and select the top 800 highly variable genes. Train a TotalVI model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=15). Calculate the final ELBO on the training set.

Load the smFISH dataset. Sort genes alphabetically. Use the first 2000 cells as the scRNA-seq reference and the remaining cells as the Spatial query. Train a CondSCVI reference model (random_seed=42, max_epochs=25). Calculate the final ELBO on the reference training set.

Load smFISH and sort genes alphabetically. Split data (Reference: first 2000 cells, Spatial: rest). Train CondSCVI (epochs=25) then train DestVI (epochs=25, random_seed=42). Calculate the final ELBO on the spatial training set.

Load smFISH, sort genes alphabetically, split (2000/Rest), and train DestVI (25 epochs each stage). Retrieve the estimated cell type proportions for the spatial spots. Calculate the mean proportion of the first cell type (index 0 in sorted categories) across all spatial spots.

Load smFISH, sort genes alphabetically, split (2000/Rest), and train DestVI (25 epochs each stage). Retrieve the estimated proportions. Identify the maximum proportion value assigned to the second cell type (index 1) in any single spot.

Load smFISH, sort genes alphabetically, split (2000/Rest), and train DestVI (25 epochs each stage). Retrieve the denoised gene expression for the first cell type (index 0). Calculate the mean expression of the first gene (index 0 in sorted genes) across all spots for this cell type.

Load smFISH, sort genes alphabetically, split (2000/Rest), and train DestVI (25 epochs each stage). Retrieve the latent representation (gamma) for the first cell type (index 0). Calculate the mean value of the first dimension (index 0) across all spatial spots.

Load smFISH, sort genes alphabetically, split (2000/Rest), and train DestVI (25 epochs each stage). Calculate the variance of the estimated proportions for the second cell type (index 1) across all spatial spots.
Load smFISH, sort genes alphabetically, split (2000/Rest), and train DestVI (25 epochs each stage). Count how many spatial spots have a proportion greater than 0.8 for the first cell type (index 0).

Load smFISH, sort genes alphabetically, split (2000/Rest), and train DestVI (25 epochs each stage). Calculate the Pearson correlation between the estimated proportion of the first cell type (index 0) and the raw spatial counts of the first gene (index 0) across all spots.

Load smFISH, sort genes alphabetically, split (2000/Rest), and train DestVI (25 epochs each stage). Retrieve the estimated proportion of the second cell type (index 1) for the spatial spot at index 100.
Perform Log-CPM normalization on the original Count matrix and calculate the maximum value in the normalized matrix.
Perform Log-CPM normalization on the original Count matrix and calculate the maximum value in the normalized matrix.

Calculate the total number of Reads (Library Size) for each sample. Use the IQR (Interquartile Range) rule to detect outliers: if the total number of Reads for a sample is less than Q1 - 1.5 * IQR or greater than Q3 + 1.5 * IQR, it will be excluded. Please return the number of remaining samples after cleaning.

Calculate the total number of Reads (Library Size) for each sample. Use the IQR (Interquartile Range) rule to detect outliers: if the total number of Reads for a sample is less than Q1 - 1.5 * IQR or greater than Q3 + 1.5 * IQR, it will be excluded. Please return the number of remaining samples after cleaning.
Given the original counts matrix, please filter out genes with counts less than 5 in more than 50% of the samples. Please return the number of genes remaining after filtering.
Given the original counts matrix, please filter out genes with counts less than 5 in more than 50% of the samples. Please return the number of genes remaining after filtering.

For this dataset, retain cells with the number of detected genes (n_genes_by_counts) between 200 and 2500 (inclusive). Please calculate the average total Count number (Total Counts) of the remaining cells after filtering.

For this dataset, retain cells with the number of detected genes (n_genes_by_counts) between 200 and 2500 (inclusive). Please calculate the average total Count number (Total Counts) of the remaining cells after filtering.

For each cell, calculate the proportion of the sum of counts of the top 50 highest expressed genes to the total counts of that cell. Mark cells with this proportion greater than 0.5 (50%) as "low complexity" and remove them. Return the total number of reads in the matrix after removal.

For each cell, calculate the proportion of the sum of counts of the top 50 highest expressed genes to the total counts of that cell. Mark cells with this proportion greater than 0.5 (50%) as "low complexity" and remove them. Return the total number of reads in the matrix after removal.
What percentage of the total number of elements in the entire expression matrix have an expression value of 0? Output a value between 0 and 1.
What percentage of the total number of elements in the entire expression matrix have an expression value of 0? Output a value between 0 and 1.
Calculate the coefficient of variation of the expression level of the gene SMARCAD1 in all samples.
Calculate the coefficient of variation of the expression level of the gene Ncor1 in all samples.
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient of the expression levels of gene CCNB1IP1 and gene AC009133.20 across all samples. Output a value between -1 and 1.
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient of the expression levels of gene Phf19 and gene Fam65b across all samples. Output a value between -1 and 1.

Perform PCA (principal component analysis) on the top 500 genes with the highest variance in the expression matrix, and output the proportion of variance explained by the first principal component (PC1). Output a value between 0 and 1.

Perform PCA (principal component analysis) on the top 500 genes with the highest variance in the expression matrix, and output the proportion of variance explained by the first principal component (PC1). Output a value between 0 and 1.

Use PCA to reduce the sample data to 50 dimensions, then use the K-Means algorithm to cluster the samples into 3 categories (k=3, random seed set to 42). Please calculate the average silhouette coefficient of the clustering results.

Use PCA to reduce the sample data to 50 dimensions, then use the K-Means algorithm to cluster the samples into 3 categories (k=3, random seed set to 42). Please calculate the average silhouette coefficient of the clustering results.
